Thanks for your review Anjella, it is valuable. 

Hi Team, 

Anjella and I decided to do these modifications:

1- Renaming @CmHandleStateGaugeMetadata to @NcmpGauge
2- Add a description field as the third to @CountCmHandleSearchExecution
3- The output file format would be:
   @AnnotationScraped: Name="", Description=""

I was talking to Daniel about this tesware and we agreed we don't need (and don't want to maintain) a unit-like-test.
however it is good idea to test/assert the output of the real script!

I recommend you ASSERT the modification/creation date/time of the output file (should be less then 1 minute ago or something like that)
AND test the number of lines in the file. it should be an expected number (~57) ie. if an instrumentation gets added this test should fail until its updated!

It is crucial when the assert fails the build fails too! Not 100% sure how to do that probably return a negative exit code form the script or similar if needed talk to team Kraken about that
this is returning object and not just ids.

I removed all unnecessary unit test but kept two:

1-The one testing the file content and the unique regex (it is handy for local development, if someone need to modify the regex in future!)
2-Test if metrics.csv was modified less than 1 minute ago and has 56 lines

https://docs.python.org/3/library/sys.html#sys.exit

This is not the name of the metric as exposed in Prometheus!
The metric name is actually on like 37: "cps_ncmp_inventory_cm_handles_by_state"

In prometheus, the metric looks like this:

    cps_ncmp_inventory_cm_handles_by_state{state=ADVISED}

The same for all the other metrics in this class. It is only a single metric with different state tags.

NB: test could (and should) use test specific names like 'custom counter name"
(AS you do in the first 2 examples)
